Diagnose Incident A as Hydrogen Sulfide with a supporting quote.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to search for chemical hazards.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser interacting with the CAMEO Chemicals website, rather than relying solely on terminal commands or prior knowledge.

integrity.description: The quotes provided in the report appear to be sourced from the CAMEO Chemicals website.
integrity.visual_evidence: The quotes in the text editor match the hazard descriptions visible in the browser screenshots for the respective chemicals.

integrity.description: The agent created the diagnosis report file in the specified location.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor or file manager shows the file 'exposure_diagnosis_report.txt' being edited or saved in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Incident A is caused by Hydrogen Sulfide (CAS 7783-06-4), which causes olfactory fatigue (loss of smell). Incident B is caused by Hydrofluoric Acid (CAS 7664-39-3), which causes delayed deep-tissue pain appearing hours after exposure. Incident C is caused by Phenol (CAS 108-95-2), which causes skin whitening and anesthetic numbness. The final report must identify these chemicals for their respective incidents and include supporting quotes from the CAMEO Chemicals website containing related keywords (e.g., 'olfactory', 'fatigue', 'delayed', 'deep', 'white', 'numb').
